criteria.description: Verifies app exists, openspec/specs/ is populated, and reports branch. Stops with guidance when specs are missing.
Builds a complete REQ inventory: capability + REQ-NNN + scenarios + keywords, including ####-level headings and compound REQ IDs
Enumerates PHP + Vue/JS/TS correctly, skipping Migration/, Db/ entity boilerplate, test files, bootstrap files

Correctly buckets: annotated/plumbing detection works, Bucket 1 uses confidence thresholds (≥0.85 or 0.70–0.85 with NEEDS-REVIEW), Bucket 2a vs 2b via capability ownership, private-helper Pass B inheritance
Reverse pass finds unimplemented REQs and splits into 3a (git log -S match) vs 3b (no history reference)
Writes both openspec/coverage-report.json (parseable, valid schema) AND openspec/coverage-report.md (human-readable)
Writes exactly two files (coverage-report.json + coverage-report.md). Never modifies code, never commits
Pauses and asks for confirmation when > 500 PHP files enumerated, or surfaces chunking guidance when Bucket 1 > 150 methods
Runs Capture Learnings step at end and routes observations through the two-stage buffer

evals.description: Small app with populated specs — full 6-bucket report
evals.setup: pipelinq has 50 PHP files across lib/Controller/, lib/Service/, lib/Listener/. openspec/specs/ has 3 capabilities with 10 REQs total. No existing coverage-report.json. Working tree may be dirty (scan is read-only so that's fine).
evals.baseline: Without the skill, Claude scans inconsistently — may produce a single markdown blob with no JSON sidecar, or omit plumbing/Bucket 4 entirely.
evals.expected_output: Writes both coverage-report.json and coverage-report.md to pipelinq/openspec/. JSON has all 8 bucket keys. MD has Summary table, Bucket 1 grouped by capability, Bucket 2a/2b with clusters, Bucket 3a/3b, Bucket 4, Notes.
assertions: reports which branch the app is on (Step 1)
reads every openspec/specs/*/spec.md and records {capability, req_id, req_title, scenarios, keywords}
enumerates PHP via Glob, skipping lib/Migration/ and lib/Db/ entity boilerplate
classifies methods into annotated / plumbing / Bucket 1 / 2a / 2b
Pass B resolves private helpers by walking callers
reverse pass splits unimplemented REQs into 3a (git log -S match) vs 3b
writes openspec/coverage-report.json matching the schema in SKILL.md Step 9
writes openspec/coverage-report.md with Summary table, all buckets, Notes section
never modifies any file other than the two report files

evals.description: App has no populated openspec/specs/ — skill must stop with guidance
evals.setup: unseeded-app exists at workspace root but openspec/specs/ is empty (no spec.md files anywhere under it).
evals.baseline: Without the skill, Claude runs the scan anyway and produces a report with 0 REQs and everything in Bucket 2 — useless output.
evals.expected_output: Stops with a message suggesting /app-explore and /app-design to seed specs before retrofit.
assertions: verifies openspec/specs/**/spec.md exists as part of Step 1 prereqs
stops when no spec.md files are found
suggests running /app-explore + /app-design to seed specs
does NOT write coverage-report.json or coverage-report.md
does NOT enumerate code units

evals.description: Target is a Python ExApp — skill must stop cleanly
evals.setup: tender-scrape is a Python ExApp wrapper: no lib/**/*.php, no src/**/*.{vue,ts,js}, only *.py files. openspec/specs/ has 2 capabilities with 4 REQs.
evals.baseline: Without the skill, Claude tries to pattern-match REQ keywords against Python AST, produces nonsense confidence scores.
evals.expected_output: Stops with Python variant deferred message after enumeration step detects only .py files.
assertions: detects only Python files during enumeration (Step 4)
stops with the explicit Python variant deferred message
does NOT attempt classification against Python code
does NOT write coverage-report.json or coverage-report.md
makes the message clear (not silent stop)

evals.description: App has > 500 PHP files — skill must pause and ask before Pass A
evals.setup: bigapp/lib/ contains 650 PHP files across many directories. openspec/specs/ has 15 capabilities and 180 REQs.
evals.baseline: Without the skill, Claude runs the full classification against 650 files, which is context-starved, or silently scopes down without telling the user.
evals.expected_output: Enumerates in Step 4, counts > 500 files, uses AskUserQuestion to confirm before Pass A classification. Offers to proceed, chunk by directory, or cancel.
assertions: reports the enumeration count (650 PHP files) to the user
compares against the > 500 threshold and triggers the Large app warning guardrail
uses AskUserQuestion to ask for confirmation before Pass A
does NOT start classification silently
if user confirms, proceeds; if user cancels, stops cleanly without writing reports

should_trigger: what code is missing spec tags
check which REQs have no implementation
run the retrofit scanner on this app
